mnemonics: DISK BOSS: Disk, I/O, Scheduling, Blocks, Organization, Structure, Services
keyPoints: Disk Scheduling Algorithms (e.g., FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN) optimize head movement.
The operating system provides services for bad-block recovery and disk formatting.
Disk structure is organized into platters, tracks, sectors, and cylinders.
commonMistakes: Confusing the SCAN (elevator) algorithm with the C-SCAN (circular scan) algorithm.
Assuming file system fragmentation is the primary cause of all slow disk performance.
Forgetting that low-level formatting is done by the manufacturer, not the OS.
